sf_incidents.narrative:
Other than an update to the highest injury severity alleged from "minor" to "moderate" and an update to reflect that property damage did result from the collision, the content of this report is unchanged from the initial report submitted on February [REDACTED], 2025 [REDACTED, MAY CONTAIN CONFIDENTIAL BUSINESS INFORMATION]. 
On February [REDACTED], 2025 at 12:10 PM PT two Waymo Autonomous Vehicles ("Waymo AVs") operating in San Francisco, CA were involved in a collision involving a cyclist on [REDACTED].
The first Waymo AV was parked at the northwest-bound curb on [REDACTED]for passenger dropoff. A second Waymo AV (2024 Jaguar I-PACE SADHW2S17R1[REDACTED], 38,609 miles) was also parked for a separate passenger dropoff, located adjacent to the first Waymo AV with the bicycle lane of northwest-bound [REDACTED] between the two Waymo AVs. A bicyclist was traveling northwest-bound on [REDACTED] and approaching the two Waymo AVs from the rear. As the cyclist was passing between the two Waymo AVs, the rear driver side passenger of the first Waymo AV opened the door of the first Waymo AV into the cyclist. The cyclist then fell into the passenger side of the second Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, both Waymo AVs' Level 4 ADS were engaged in autonomous mode. Neither Waymo AV sustained damage. The cyclist was able to move out of the roadway independently and was then transported from the scene in an ambulance. 
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because an individual involved was transported from the scene to a hospital for medical treatment. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

Since the initial report [REDACTED, MAY CONTAIN CONFIDENTIAL BUSINESS INFORMATION] was submitted on January 20, 2025, the content of the report has been clarified with changes pertaining to the other vehicles involved and the zip code at which the collision occurred.
On January [REDACTED], 2025 at 6:07 PM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle (Waymo AV) operating in San Francisco, CA was in a collision involving a passenger vehicle near the intersection of [REDACTED] and [REDACTED].
The Waymo AV, which had no occupants, was traveling northwestbound in the middle of three lanes on [REDACTED] and came to a stop in a queue of traffic. Shortly after, a passenger vehicle came to a stop behind the Waymo AV. While the Waymo AV and the other passenger vehicle were stopped, an SUV approached from behind at an extreme rate of speed and made contact with the passenger vehicle behind the Waymo AV, which then made contact with the rear bumper of the Waymo AV. According to the San Francisco Police Department, the Waymo AV then began to rotate clockwise and, as it was rotating, the front of the SUV made contact with the passenger side of the Waymo AV. The rear of the Waymo AV then made contact with the rear driver side corner of another passenger car that had just begun to proceed straight on northbound [REDACTED]. According to the San Francisco Police Department, at least two other vehicles were involved in the crash and one of the occupants of the vehicles involved in the crash and a domestic animal were declared deceased at the scene. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AVs Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. The Waymo AV, the passenger vehicle behind the Waymo AV, and the SUV sustained severe damage. The extent of the damage to the other three vehicles is currently unknown. Waymo received notice that five passengers in four of the vehicles involved sustained injuries of varying severity. 
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because of a fatality. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

The content of this report is unchanged from the initial report submitted on October 29, 2024 [REDACTED, MAY CONTAIN CONFIDENTIAL BUSINESS INFORMATION]. 
On October [REDACTED], 2024 at 8:52 AM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in San Francisco, California was in a collision involving a SUV on [REDACTED] at [REDACTED]. 
The Waymo AV came to a stop in a queue of traffic for a red traffic light in the rightmost lane of the two eastbound lanes on [REDACTED] at the intersection with [REDACTED].  A passenger car traveling west on [REDACTED] crossed the double yellow line and made contact with an SUV that was alongside the Waymo AV in the left lane of eastbound [REDACTED].  The impact caused the passenger side of the SUV to make contact with the driver side of the Waymo AV.  At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode.  All three vehicles sustained damage.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because an individual involved was transported from the scene to a hospital for medical treatment. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

On June [REDACTED], 2024 at 11:31 PM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in San Francisco, California was in a collision involving a passenger car on [REDACTED] at [REDACTED]. 
The Waymo AV was traveling south on [REDACTED] and stopped at a stop sign at [REDACTED].  After the stop as the Waymo AV started to enter the intersection, a passenger car traveling behind the Waymo AV made contact with the rear bumper of the Waymo AV.  At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. Both vehicles sustained damage.  
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 a vehicle involved was towed away. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

Other than correcting the Mileage to 64,204, adding law enforcement investigation details, adding the ADS Software Version, and adding the Waymo AVs Pre-Crash Speed, the content of this report is unchanged from the initial report submitted on November 4, 2023 [REDACTED, MAY CONTAIN CONFIDENTIAL BUSINESS INFORMATION]. 
On November [REDACTED], 2023 at 10:43 PM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle (Waymo AV) operating in San Francisco, California was in a collision involving a passenger car on [REDACTED] at [REDACTED]. 
The Waymo AV was traveling northbound in the left lane on [REDACTED] and stopped at a red light at the intersection with [REDACTED] alongside a passenger vehicle in the right lane. After the light turned green, both the Waymo AV and the adjacent passenger car proceeded into the intersection. While in the intersection, a passenger car traveling west on [REDACTED] ran the red light and the front left corner and left side of this vehicle made contact with the front right of the Waymo AV and the front of the adjacent passenger car. After impact, the vehicle that ran the red light struck pedestrians that had been standing on the sidewalk on the northwest corner of the intersection. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AVs Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. All three vehicles sustained damage and were towed from the scene.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because an individual involved was transported from the scene to a hospital for medical treatment, and a vehicle involved was towed away. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

This report includes removal of a reference to the Waymo AV sustaining damage (upon further inspection, the Waymo AV was not damaged) and an update reflecting an alleged injury that Waymo received notice of on June 10, 2025. The remaining content of this report is unchanged from the initial report submitted on June 5, 2025 [REDACTED, MAY CONTAIN CONFIDENTIAL BUSINESS INFORMATION]. 
On June [REDACTED], 2025 at 7:50 AM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in San Francisco, California was in a collision involving a scooterist on [REDACTED] at [REDACTED]. 
The Waymo AV was traveling northbound in the rightmost lane on [REDACTED] and came to a stop in a queue of traffic for a red light at the intersection with [REDACTED]. While the Waymo AV was stopped, the passenger in the Waymo AV opened the rear passenger side door. A scooterist traveling northbound on [REDACTED] proceeded to pass to the right of the Waymo AV, and the scooterist made contact with the open rear passenger door of the stationary Waymo AV. The passenger was not belted at the time of the collision, as the passenger was preparing to exit the Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. There was an allegation of damage to the scooter.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because a vulnerable road user was involved. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

sf_incidents.address: Frida Kahlo Way near Cloud Circle
sf_incidents.narrative:
On August [REDACTED], 2025 at 6:06 AM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in San Francisco, California was in a collision involving an SUV on [REDACTED] near [REDACTED]. 
The Waymo AV was parked at the curb facing south on [REDACTED] when an SUV traveling northbound on [REDACTED] crossed into the southbound lane of traffic. The SUV continued towards the Waymo AV at a high rate of speed, and the front left of the SUV made contact with the front left of the stationary Waymo AV. The SUV then continued to proceed northbound in the southbound lane of [REDACTED], coming to a stop after making contact with a sign post at the intersection with [REDACTED]. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. Both vehicles sustained damage and the driver of the passenger car was transported to a hospital for medical treatment.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because an individual involved was transported from the scene to a hospital for medical treatment, vehicles involved were towed away, and because of airbag deployment. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

all_incidents.address: N. 92nd Street near E. Cochise Drive
all_incidents.narrative:
On November [REDACTED], 2025 at 12:02 AM MT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in Scottsdale, Arizona was in a collision involving a passenger of the Waymo AV on [REDACTED] near [REDACTED].
The Waymo AV was travelling northbound in the left lane of [REDACTED]. While the AV was in  motion, the two passengers seated in the rear of the Waymo AV removed their seatbelts and opened the right rear passenger side door. The Waymo AV detected the opened door and began to slow in lane. While the Waymo AV was slowing to a stop, the passenger seated in the right rear passenger seat exited the vehicle through the open door, placed their feet on the ground, and the rear right tire of the Waymo AV came to a stop on top of the passenger's right foot. The Waymo AV remained stopped on top of the passenger's foot until emergency services arrived and lifted the right side of the vehicle. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. The passenger of the Waymo AV was transported from the scene to a hospital for medical treatment with moderate injuries to the foot.

Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because an individual involved was transported from the scene to a hospital for medical treatment and a vulnerable road user was struck by an involved vehicle. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

all_incidents.address: James P Brawley Drive at Carter St NW
all_incidents.narrative:
On October [REDACTED], 2025 at 9:55 AM EDT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in Atlanta, Georgia was in a collision involving a passenger car on [REDACTED] at [REDACTED]. 
The Waymo AV was traveling northbound on [REDACTED] and came to a stop at the stop sign at the intersection with [REDACTED]. A passenger car traveling directly behind the Waymo AV proceeded without stopping, and the front driver side of the passenger car then made contact with the rear side of the stationary Waymo AV. First responders arrived at the scene and assisted the rider out of the Waymo AV. An ambulance was called to the scene and Waymo later learned that the rider was transported to the hospital. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. The Waymo AV sustained damage. The passenger in the Waymo AV was not belted at the time of the collision they were in the process of locating the belt fastener for the seat they were occupying.

Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because an individual involved was transported from the scene to a hospital for medical treatment. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

all_incidents.address: Rural Road at E Lemon Street
all_incidents.narrative:
On September [REDACTED], 2025 at 1:21 AM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in Tempe, Arizona was in a collision involving a motorcycle on [REDACTED] at [REDACTED]. 
The Waymo AV was traveling north in the rightmost lane on [REDACTED] and began slowing with its right turn signal activated in preparation for a right turn into a parking lot at [REDACTED], yielding to a pedestrian crossing the driveway entrance to the parking lot on the right-hand sidewalk. While the Waymo AV was slowing with its right turn signal activated, a motorcycle in the rightmost lane approached the Waymo AV from behind and the front of the motorcycle made contact with the rear left corner of the Waymo AV. The impact with the Waymo AV resulted in the motorcyclist falling from the motorcycle and into the roadway to the left of the Waymo AV. Immediately afterwards, a passenger car in the second-from-the-rightmost lane passed the Waymo AV on the left and struck the motorcyclist in the roadway. The passenger car did not stop and left the scene. The motorcyclist was transported from the scene to a hospital, where they were declared deceased. At the time of impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode, and the Waymo AV was not carrying passengers. Following contact with the motorcycle, the Waymo AV came to a complete stop and remained stopped until the vehicle was towed away several hours later. The Waymo AV and the motorcycle sustained damage. The extent of the damage to the passenger car that left the scene is unknown at this time.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because of a fatality. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

all_incidents.address: E Indian School Road at N 38th Street
all_incidents.narrative:
The content of this report is unchanged from the initial report submitted on February [REDACTED], 2025 [REDACTED, MAY CONTAIN CONFIDENTIAL BUSINESS INFORMATION]. 
On February [REDACTED], 2025 at 1:39 PM MT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in Phoenix, Arizona was in a collision involving a passenger car on [REDACTED] at [REDACTED]. 
The Waymo AV was traveling eastbound on [REDACTED] in the dedicated left turn lane preparing to make an unprotected left turn onto [REDACTED] when it came to a stop behind a passenger car also waiting to make an unprotected left turn. While the Waymo AV remained stopped, the passenger car proceeded to make an unprotected left turn onto [REDACTED], crossing in front of an oncoming SUV traveling west on [REDACTED]. The passenger car was struck on its passenger side by the oncoming SUV, causing the passenger car to rotate counter-clockwise. After impact, the passenger car then continued turning left, circling back towards the Waymo AV. The front passenger side of the passenger car then made contact with the front driver side of the stationary Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. Both vehicles sustained damage. A passenger in the Waymo AV and the driver of the passenger car were transported to the hospital from the scene in an ambulance.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because an individual involved was transported from the scene to a hospital for medical treatment. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

all_incidents.address: S. Grand Avenue near W. Pico Boulevard
all_incidents.narrative:
Other than updating the highest injury severity alleged from "unknown" to "moderate", the content of this report is unchanged from the initial report submitted on February [REDACTED], 2025 [REDACTED, MAY CONTAIN CONFIDENTIAL BUSINESS INFORMATION]. 
On February [REDACTED], 2025 at 9:56 PM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in Los Angeles, California was in a collision involving an SUV on [REDACTED]. 
The Waymo AV was traveling southwest in the rightmost lane of [REDACTED]with its right turn signal activated, preparing to proceed into an upcoming dedicated right turn lane prior to the intersection with [REDACTED] While the Waymo AV proceeded into the right turn lane, an SUV traveling in the rightmost lane approached the Waymo AV from behind, and the front of the SUV made contact with the rear of the Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. The SUV left the scene and reportedly was involved in a subsequent collision approximately one block away. Both the Waymo AV and the SUV sustained damage, and three passengers in the Waymo AV were transported to a hospital for medical treatment.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because an individual involved was transported from the scene to a hospital for medical treatment. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

all_incidents.address: N Dobson Rd and W Flint Street
all_incidents.narrative:
The content of this report is unchanged from the initial report submitted on March [REDACTED], 2025 [REDACTED, MAY CONTAIN CONFIDENTIAL BUSINESS INFORMATION]. 
On February [REDACTED], 2025 at 3:39 AM MT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in Chandler, Arizona was in a collision involving an SUV on [REDACTED]. 
The Waymo AV was traveling northbound on [REDACTED] in the left lane towards the intersection of [REDACTED]. An SUV traveling northbound in the right lane passed the Waymo AV on the right and continued into the dedicated right turn lane as it approached [REDACTED] Street. The SUV crossed into the intersection with W. Flint Street from the dedicated right turn lane and continued traveling straight onto the far-side sidewalk. The SUV then departed the roadway, striking a fire hydrant and a utility bollard before making contact with a street light. The impact with the street light resulted in the SUV coming to a stop and re-entering the roadway on [REDACTED] in the Waymo AV's path of travel. The rear side of the SUV made contact with the front passenger side corner of the Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. Both vehicles sustained damage. The driver of the SUV was transported to a local hospital for treatment. One of the two passengers in the Waymo AV reported minor injuries but refused medical treatment. Both passengers, who had been seated in the rear of the Waymo AV, were not belted at the time of the collision, having had buckled their belts behind them.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because an individual involved was transported from the scene to a hospital for medical treatment and a vehicle involved was towed away. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

The Waymo AV slowed to a stop to yield to traffic traveling east on [REDACTED] as it prepared to make a right turn onto [REDACTED].
